commit.message:
Add host_wake_all_slots to pre-warm shadow FX before Song Mode playback

With Link Audio, Move's native track audio routes through shadow slots
for FX processing. When a track goes quiet, the slot idles and skips
rendering. On step transitions, the sudden audio arrival caused glitches
because FX state was stale. Wake all slots at playback start and each
step advance so FX chains run on zeros before audio arrives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add code rehydration pipeline for two-VM code sync demo

Implements the four prerequisites for demonstrating live code sync
between two running Maggie instances:

1. Code rehydration: pipeline/rehydrate.go compiles synced content
   from ContentStore into runnable classes via 5-phase pipeline
   (collect → topo-sort → skeletons → compile methods → re-index)

2. Enriched distribution chunks: class chunks now carry full structural
   metadata (namespace, superclass, ivars) via line-oriented text format;
   method chunks carry selector, class name, and class-side flag

3. Disk cache: vm/dist/cache.go persists content-addressed chunks to
   .maggie/cache/ with git-style 2-char prefix directories and atomic writes

4. Visibility commands: mag sync list/diff/show for inspecting local
   content store and comparing with peers; Resolve and List RPCs for
   class-name-based pull and content enumeration

Also adds ParseMethodDef to compiler for standalone method parsing,
and removes Yaegi references from future-phases docs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

macOS sed has different syntax for the insert command. Using node -e

commit.message:
Fix commit message GHA syntax and changelog printf (#26)

## Summary

- **Fixed commit message**: Used bash `${}` instead of GHA `${{ }}` for
`pages_written` and `pai_sha` — these expanded to empty strings,
producing broken commit messages like `"docs: regenerate pages from PAI
"`
- **Fixed SHA truncation**: `${inputs.pai_sha:0:7}` (bash substring on
non-existent var) replaced with `$(echo "${{ inputs.pai_sha }}" | cut
-c1-7)`
- **Hardened changelog entry**: Switched `echo -e` to `printf '%b'` for
more robust escape sequence handling

## Verified working (no changes needed)

- sed pattern for footer `Site vX.Y.Z` replacement ✓
- Changelog `## ` heading detection at line 13 ✓
- `NEW_VERSION` env var propagation between steps ✓
- `.regeneration-result.json` field names match jq queries ✓

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify no remaining `${lowercase...}` GHA expression mistakes
(`grep` confirmed clean)
- [ ] Verify build passes (`npm run build` succeeded)
- [ ] After merge + #24 pipeline fix, trigger watcher to validate full
end-to-end

Closes #25

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Close the self-evolution loop + expand to regular user use cases

1. Fix trajectory data feed (closes the self-evolution loop)
   - ExtractionAgent now stores html (truncated to 50k chars) and url
     alongside target_schema in action_params for every llm_extract record
   - EvolutionLoop.run_cycle() builds real IOPair objects from these stored
     records and passes them to SkillForge.forge(sample_io=...)
   - Previously the synthesizer received zero examples; now it gets the
     actual HTML it needs to generate a working extraction function

2. Consumer-oriented skill library (5 new skills, 22 new domains)
   - GitHubRepoSkill: name, description, language, stars, forks, license, topics
   - ArxivPaperSkill: title, authors, abstract, submission date, subjects
   - RedditThreadSkill: title, subreddit, score, comments, top 3 comments
   - RecipeSkill: name, ingredients, times, servings, calories (schema.org JSON-LD)
   - ProductPageSkill: name, price, currency, availability, rating (schema.org JSON-LD)
   - Expanded article domains: BBC, NYT, Guardian, Reuters, Bloomberg, WSJ + more
   - Skills answer questions users have ("how much does this cost?") not just
     "what metadata is on this page?"

3. Cross-session memory (MemoryStore + remember/recall tools)
   - New agent_memory DB table (namespace + key-value, with upsert)
   - MemoryStore: set/get/delete/list_keys with namespace isolation
   - RememberTool: agent can persist facts across sessions
   - RecallTool: agent can look up previously remembered values or list all keys
   - Both wired into bootstrap and always registered

4. Browser automation path (documented, not yet implemented)
   - playwright added to optional [browser] group in pyproject.toml
   - enable_browser_fetch config flag + EVOSYS_ENABLE_BROWSER_FETCH env var
   - Most consumer shopping/news sites require JS rendering — this is the
     clearly documented next step when static HTML coverage is insufficient

Tests: 500 passing (454 existing + 46 new covering all additions)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Production safeguards: backtest validation, edge cap, data staleness

Step 1 - Backtest validation with pass/fail verdict:
- Added production_readiness_report() with clear PASS/FAIL/CONDITIONAL verdict
- Simulated betting ROI against synthetic market lines
- Human-readable format_report() with calibration curves, monthly P&L,
  drawdown tracking, and actionable next steps
- Thresholds: Brier < 0.24, accuracy > 52%, log loss < 0.69

Step 2 - Max edge cap (default 10pp):
- Edges above max_edge are rejected as model artifacts, not opportunities
- NHL books are sharp; 10+pp edges almost always mean the model is wrong
- Configurable via --max-edge CLI flag and TrackerConfig.max_edge

Step 3 - Data staleness detection:
- MarketSnapshot now tracks fetched_at, odds_source, strength_source
- check_data_freshness() returns warnings for stale/degraded data
- score_snapshot() blocks recommendations on critical data issues
  (empty odds, empty strength, data > 6 hours old)
- Warns on bulk CSV fallback and low team count
- CLI surfaces warnings to stderr before showing picks

12 new tests (228 total), all passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

queries using { count: 'exact', head: true } which returns only the
count without fetching any row data.

commit.message:
fix(watchdog): cover pre-PR zombies, conflict state mismatch, and stuck merge queue (#256)

* fix(watchdog): cover pre-PR zombies, conflict state mismatch, and stuck merge queue

Closes #255

- Gap 1: scan claimed WorkBeads with no pr_id and elapsed > timeout;
  log zombie_detected with reason="pre_pr_zombie" then unclaim so the
  scheduler can re-queue the issue.
- Gap 2: when a PRBead has state="conflict", reset fix_attempts=0 and
  state="open" instead of dispatching a recovery agent; log conflict_reset
  and let _resume_stale_issues handle retry.
- Gap 3: after both bead loops, check the merge queue head; if it has been
  enqueued longer than WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MINUTES log a human_escalate event.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rework L4 from categories to activities with auto-generated questions

L4 now stores a list of activities (e.g., Music, Swimming, Archery).
Two are randomly picked and 4 questions are generated from the pair:
- Why do you prefer A over B?
- Why do you prefer B over A?
- Why do you want to do both A and B?
- Why do you want to do neither A or B?

Supports batch import for quickly adding all 50 activities at once.

commit.message:
fix: debrief timing race + null checkin_id drops + check-in gate on launch

Bug 1: Add 1500ms settling delay after kovaaks-sync full_sync before
querying score_history, so DB writes propagate before the read.

Bug 2A: Use explicit FK hint for mental_checkins join in useSessionNotes
so debriefs with null checkin_id are not silently dropped by inner join.

Bug 2B: Add forceShow() to usePreTrainingGate that bypasses cooldown.

Bug 2C: Wire forceShow through Dashboard → Training so the check-in
modal fires once when first launching a training program per session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(dashboard): stack commands run directly without self-SSH

The pipeline scripts SSH to 10.0.0.10 from Mac Mini, but
runRemoteCommand executes ON the Linux Desktop — causing the
scripts to SSH to themselves. Replace with direct inline commands
that run locally on the desktop.

commit.message:
feat: bootstrap MVP monorepo structure

- Monorepo with npm workspaces: apps/ (api, agents) + packages/ (types, ai, memory, evolution, db)
- Fastify API server (port 3000) with /health and POST /webhook/message
- BullMQ worker for async message processing with retry/backoff
- 5 AI agents: Vera (attendant), Cat (categorizer), Cy (copywriter), Leo (analyst), Atlas (CEO)
- PostgreSQL schema (7 tables): tenants, contacts, conversations, messages, properties, lead_profiles, agent_logs
- pgvector extension for property embedding search
- Redis memory layer for conversation context caching
- Evolution API v2 HTTP client (sendText, sendMedia, presence)
- Anthropic Claude + OpenAI GPT-4o wrappers with token tracking
- PM2 config for API (cluster x2) + worker (fork x1)
- Full TypeScript with strict mode, no any types
- Subpath exports for @imobx/ai package (claude, openai)

commit.message:
feat(m3): CUPED covariate computation (milestone 1.12) (#9)

* feat(m3): add CUPED covariate computation (milestone 1.12)

Compute pre-experiment metric values as CUPED covariates for variance
reduction by M4a. For metrics with cuped_covariate_metric_id configured,
queries the 7-day pre-experiment window from metric_events and writes
the covariate to metric_summaries.cuped_covariate. All CUPED SQL queries
are logged to query_log with job_type "cuped_covariate".

This unblocks Agent-4 M1.15 (CUPED variance reduction).

commit.message:
fix(rolling): ftesEfectivos prioriza ftesReales independientemente de fuente

fuente es determinado por revenueReal en el backend. Cuando un mes
tiene ftesReales pero no revenueReal (o era 0), fuente quedaba como
ASIGNADO y ftesEfectivos usaba ftesAsignados, pero backlog ya usaba
ftesReales → discrepancia.

Fix: ftesEfectivos verifica ftesReales != null directamente, sin
depender de fuente. Mantiene el invariante total = backlog + potencial
en todos los casos.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
